metadata.description: Model/deployment name as used in the API URL (e.g., "gpt-4o", "gpt-4.1-mini"). This MUST match the actual deployment name in Azure OpenAI — all instances serving this model must use the same name.
PTU capacity in tokens per minute for this deployment. Required on PTU instances. Multiple PTU deployments for the same model across instances are summed.
A model deployment within a Foundry instance. Deployment name = modelName (enforced convention).
The value to match against the JWT claim (prefix match — exact values are a trivial prefix). E.g., a client_id or a managed identity resource ID prefix.
Human-readable label for this identity (for logging/diagnostics)
The JWT claim name to match against. Common values: "azp", "appid", "xms_mirid", "oid", "sub".
An identity matcher that maps a JWT claim to a contract.
Model/deployment name (e.g., "gpt-4o", "gpt-4.1-mini")
Total tokens per minute for this model (hard 429 limit). Controls fair sharing of this model across teams.
Max TPM routed to PTU for this model (soft limit — overflow to PAYG). Must be ≤ tpm. Set 0 or omit for PAYG-only.
Per-model throughput and PTU allocation for a team.
Human-readable team/app name (e.g., "Team Alpha Production"). Also used as the shared counter-key for quota enforcement across all identities in this contract.

Identity matchers — each entry specifies a JWT claim name and a value to prefix-match against it. Multiple identities can share a single contract (e.g., an app registration + a managed identity for the same team).
Routing priority: 1=Production (PTU first), 2=Standard (PTU when idle), 3=Batch (PAYG only)
Per-model TPM and PTU allocations. Also serves as the allow-list — models not listed are denied.
Monthly token budget across ALL models (general cost cap). Hard limit — 429 if exceeded.
Environment tag for observability (e.g., PROD, DEV, TEST)
Access contract defining a team/app identity, priority, and quota.
Friendly name for this instance (used in backend naming, e.g., "foundry-eastus-ptu")
Full Azure resource ID: /subscriptions/.../Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/<name>
Endpoint URL, e.g. https://my-foundry.openai.azure.com/
Azure region where the instance is deployed
Whether ALL deployments on this instance use Provisioned Throughput Units. Instance must be PTU-only or paygo-only.
Model deployments on this instance. Deployment name = modelName (convention enforced).
Priority in backend pool (1=local PTU, 2=remote PTU, 3=paygo). Lower = preferred. Within a pool, APIM tries all backends at priority N before moving to priority N+1.
Weight for load balancing within the same priority tier
Reference to an existing Azure AI Services / Foundry instance. Must be PTU-only or paygo-only.
Array of existing Foundry/OpenAI instances to register as backends
Access contracts defining team identities, priorities, and quotas
P2 routing threshold — route to PTU when utilization < this value (0.0–1.0)

metadata.name: Event Hub Namespaces
metadata.description: This module deploys an Event Hub Namespace.
The name of the private endpoint.
The resource ID of the private endpoint.
The group Id for the private endpoint Group.
FQDN that resolves to private endpoint IP address.
A list of private IP addresses of the private endpoint.
The custom DNS configurations of the private endpoint.
The IDs of the network interfaces associated with the private endpoint.
Required. The name of the disaster recovery config.
Optional. Resource ID of the Primary/Secondary event hub namespace name, which is part of GEO DR pairing.
Required. The resource ID of the key vault where to store the secrets of this module.
Optional. The rootPrimaryConnectionStringName secret name to create.
Optional. The rootSecondaryConnectionStringName secret name to create.
Optional. The rootPrimaryKeyName secret name to create.
Optional. The rootSecondaryKeyName secret name to create.
Required. The name of the Event Hub.
Optional. Authorization Rules for the Event Hub.
Optional. Number of days to retain the events for this Event Hub, value should be 1 to 7 days. Will be automatically set to infinite retention if cleanup policy is set to "Compact".
Optional. Number of partitions created for the Event Hub, allowed values are from 1 to 32 partitions.
Optional. Enumerates the possible values for the status of the Event Hub.
Optional. The consumer groups to create in this Event Hub instance.
Optional. The lock settings of the service.
Optional. Array of role assignments to create.
Optional. Properties of capture description.
Optional. A value that indicates whether to enable retention description properties. If it is set to true the messageRetentionInDays property is ignored.
Optional. Retention cleanup policy. Enumerates the possible values for cleanup policy.

Optional. Retention time in hours. Number of hours to retain the events for this Event Hub. This value is only used when cleanupPolicy is Delete and it overrides the messageRetentionInDays. If cleanupPolicy is Compact the returned value of this property is Long.MaxValue.

Optional. Retention cleanup policy. Number of hours to retain the tombstone markers of a compacted Event Hub. This value is only used when cleanupPolicy is Compact. Consumer must complete reading the tombstone marker within this specified amount of time if consumer begins from starting offset to ensure they get a valid snapshot for the specific key described by the tombstone marker within the compacted Event Hub.
The type of an event hub.

Optional. This determines if traffic is allowed over public network. Default is "Enabled". If set to "Disabled", traffic to this namespace will be restricted over Private Endpoints only and network rules will not be applied.

Optional. Default Action for Network Rule Set. Default is "Allow". It will not be set if publicNetworkAccess is "Disabled". Otherwise, it will be set to "Deny" if ipRules or virtualNetworkRules are being used.
Optional. Value that indicates whether Trusted Service Access is enabled or not.

Optional. An array of subnet resource ID objects that this Event Hub Namespace is exposed to via Service Endpoints. You can enable the `ignoreMissingVnetServiceEndpoint` if you wish to add this virtual network to Event Hub Namespace but do not have an existing service endpoint. It will not be set if publicNetworkAccess is "Disabled". Otherwise, when used, defaultAction will be set to "Deny".

Optional. An array of objects for the public IP ranges you want to allow via the Event Hub Namespace firewall. Supports IPv4 address or CIDR. It will not be set if publicNetworkAccess is "Disabled". Otherwise, when used, defaultAction will be set to "Deny".
Optional. The name of the network ruleset. Defaults to 'default'.
The type of a network rule set.
Optional. FQDN that resolves to private endpoint IP address.
Required. A list of private IP addresses of the private endpoint.
Required. The name of the resource that is unique within a resource group.
Required. The ID of a group obtained from the remote resource that this private endpoint should connect to.
Required. The member name of a group obtained from the remote resource that this private endpoint should connect to.
Required. A private IP address obtained from the private endpoint's subnet.
Required. Properties of private endpoint IP configurations.
Optional. The name of the Private DNS Zone Group.
Optional. The name of the private DNS Zone Group config.
Required. The resource id of the private DNS zone.
Required. The private DNS Zone Groups to associate the Private Endpoint. A DNS Zone Group can support up to 5 DNS zones.
The resourceId of the exported secret.
The secret URI of the exported secret.
The secret URI with version of the exported secret.
An AVM-aligned type for the output of the secret set via the secrets export feature.
Optional. The identity used for the capture destination.
Optional. Name for capture destination.
Optional. Properties describing the storage account, blob container and archive name format for capture destination.
Optional. Properties of Destination where capture will be stored. (Storage Account, Blob Names).
Optional. A value that indicates whether capture description is enabled. Defaults to true if `captureDescription` is provided.
Optional. Enumerates the possible values for the encoding format of capture description. Note: "AvroDeflate" will be deprecated in New API Version.
Optional. The time window allows you to set the frequency with which the capture to Azure Blobs will happen.
Optional. The size window defines the amount of data built up in your Event Hub before an capture operation.
Optional. A value that indicates whether to Skip Empty Archives.
The type of a capture description.
Required. The name of the consumer group.

Optional. User Metadata is a placeholder to store user-defined string data with maximum length 1024. e.g. it can be used to store descriptive data, such as list of teams and their contact information also user-defined configuration settings can be stored.
Type definition for an Event Hub consumer group.
Required. The resource ID of a key vault to reference a customer managed key for encryption from.
Required. The name of the customer managed key to use for encryption.
Optional. The version of the customer managed key to reference for encryption. If not provided, using version as per 'autoRotationEnabled' setting.
Optional. Enable or disable auto-rotating to the latest key version. Default is `true`. If set to `false`, the latest key version at the time of the deployment is used.
Optional. User assigned identity to use when fetching the customer managed key. Required if no system assigned identity is available for use.
An AVM-aligned type for a customer-managed key. To be used if the resource type supports auto-rotation of the customer-managed key.
Optional. The name of the diagnostic setting.
Optional. Name of a Diagnostic Log category for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set the specific logs to collect here.
Optional. Name of a Diagnostic Log category group for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set to `allLogs` to collect all logs.
Optional. Enable or disable the category explicitly. Default is `true`.
Optional. The name of logs that will be streamed. "allLogs" includes all possible logs for the resource. Set to `[]` to disable log collection.
Required. Name of a Diagnostic Metric category for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set to `AllMetrics` to collect all metrics.
Optional. Enable or disable the category explicitly. Default is `true`.
Optional. The name of metrics that will be streamed. "allMetrics" includes all possible metrics for the resource. Set to `[]` to disable metric collection.
Optional. A string indicating whether the export to Log Analytics should use the default destination type, i.e. AzureDiagnostics, or use a destination type.

Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic log analytics workspace. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.

Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic storage account. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.
Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic event hub authorization rule for the Event Hubs namespace in which the event hub should be created or streamed to.

Optional. Name of the diagnostic event hub within the namespace to which logs are streamed. Without this, an event hub is created for each log category. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.
Optional. The full ARM resource ID of the Marketplace resource to which you would like to send Diagnostic Logs.
An AVM-aligned type for a diagnostic setting. To be used if both logs & metrics are supported by the resource provider.
Required. The name of the Event Hub authorization rule.

Optional. Specify the type of lock.
Optional. Specify the notes of the lock.
An AVM-aligned type for a lock.

Optional. The resource ID to assign to the resource. Mutually exclusive with `systemAssigned`.

Optional. The resource Id of a Virtual Network Subnet.

Optional. The name of the private link connection to create.

Optional. The custom name of the network interface attached to the Private Endpoint.
Optional. Specify the type of lock.

Optional. Tags to be applied on all resources/Resource Groups in this deployment.
Optional. Enable/Disable usage telemetry for module.

An AVM-aligned type for a private endpoint. To be used if the private endpoint's default service / groupId can be assumed (i.e., for services that only have one Private Endpoint type like 'vault' for key vault).
Optional. The name (as GUID) of the role assignment. If not provided, a GUID will be generated.

Required. The role to assign. You can provide either the display name of the role definition, the role definition GUID, or its fully qualified ID in the following format: '/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions/c2f4ef07-c644-48eb-af81-4b1b4947fb11'.
Required. The principal ID of the principal (user/group/identity) to assign the role to.

Optional. Version of the condition.
Optional. The Resource Id of the delegated managed identity resource.
An AVM-aligned type for a role assignment.
An exported secret's references.
A map of the exported secrets
Required. The name of the event hub namespace.
Optional. Location for all resources.
Optional. event hub plan SKU name.

Optional. The Event Hubs throughput units for Basic or Standard tiers, where value should be 0 to 20 throughput units. The Event Hubs premium units for Premium tier, where value should be 0 to 10 premium units.
Optional. Switch to make the Event Hub Namespace zone redundant.
Optional. Switch to enable the Auto Inflate feature of Event Hub. Auto Inflate is not supported in Premium SKU EventHub.
Optional. Upper limit of throughput units when AutoInflate is enabled, value should be within 0 to 20 throughput units.
Optional. Authorization Rules for the Event Hub namespace.
Optional. This property disables SAS authentication for the Event Hubs namespace.
Optional. Value that indicates whether Kafka is enabled for Event Hubs Namespace.
Optional. The minimum TLS version for the cluster to support.
Optional. Whether or not public network access is allowed for this resource. For security reasons it should be disabled. If not specified, it will be disabled by default if private endpoints are set.
Optional. Configuration details for private endpoints. For security reasons, it is recommended to use private endpoints whenever possible.

Optional. The diagnostic settings of the service.

Optional. The managed identity definition for this resource.

Optional. Array of role assignments to create.

Optional. The event hubs to deploy into this namespace.
Optional. The disaster recovery config for this namespace.
Optional. Key vault reference and secret settings for the module's secrets export.

metadata.name: Event Hub Namespace Network Rule Sets
metadata.description: This module deploys an Event Hub Namespace Network Rule Set.
Optional. The resource Id of a Virtual Network Subnet.
Optional. Value that indicates whether to ignore missing Vnet Service Endpoint.
Conditional. The name of the parent event hub namespace. Required if the template is used in a standalone deployment.

Optional. This determines if traffic is allowed over public network. Default is "Enabled". If set to "Disabled", traffic to this namespace will be restricted over Private Endpoints only and network rules will not be applied.

Optional. Default Action for Network Rule Set. Default is "Allow". It will not be set if publicNetworkAccess is "Disabled". Otherwise, it will be set to "Deny" if ipRules or virtualNetworkRules are being used.
Optional. Value that indicates whether Trusted Service Access is enabled or not.

Optional. An array of subnet resource ID objects that this Event Hub Namespace is exposed to via Service Endpoints. You can enable the `ignoreMissingVnetServiceEndpoint` if you wish to add this virtual network to Event Hub Namespace but do not have an existing service endpoint. It will not be set if publicNetworkAccess is "Disabled". Otherwise, when used, defaultAction will be set to "Deny".

Optional. An array of objects for the public IP ranges you want to allow via the Event Hub Namespace firewall. Supports IPv4 address or CIDR. It will not be set if publicNetworkAccess is "Disabled". Otherwise, when used, defaultAction will be set to "Deny".
Optional. The name of the network ruleset.
The name of the network rule set.

variables.$fxv#0:
<policies>
    <inbound>
        <base />

        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <!-- STEP 1: Validate Bearer Token against Entra ID                   -->
        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <validate-azure-ad-token
            tenant-id="{tenant-id}"
            header-name="Authorization"
            failed-validation-httpcode="401"
            failed-validation-error-message="Unauthorized. A valid Entra ID bearer token is required."
            output-token-variable-name="jwt">
            <audiences>
                <!-- Use Cognitive Services audience so Foundry-integrated clients work natively -->
                <audience>https://cognitiveservices.azure.com</audience>
            </audiences>
        </validate-azure-ad-token>

        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <!-- STEP 2: Extract caller identity claims from JWT                    -->
        <!--                                                                    -->
        <!-- Different identity types surface in different claims:              -->
        <!--   azp/appid  — app registrations (service principal tokens)        -->
        <!--   xms_mirid  — managed identities (full Azure resource ID)         -->
        <!--   oid        — object ID (fallback for any Entra identity)         -->
        <!-- We extract all available claims; the identity lookup will match    -->
        <!-- against the correct one based on each identity's claimName.        -->
        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <set-variable name="claim-azp" value="@(((Jwt)context.Variables["jwt"]).Claims.GetValueOrDefault("azp", ""))" />
        <set-variable name="claim-appid" value="@(((Jwt)context.Variables["jwt"]).Claims.GetValueOrDefault("appid", ""))" />
        <set-variable name="claim-xms_mirid" value="@(((Jwt)context.Variables["jwt"]).Claims.GetValueOrDefault("xms_mirid", ""))" />
        <set-variable name="claim-oid" value="@(((Jwt)context.Variables["jwt"]).Claims.GetValueOrDefault("oid", ""))" />
        <set-variable name="claim-sub" value="@(((Jwt)context.Variables["jwt"]).Claims.GetValueOrDefault("sub", ""))" />

        <!-- Build a caller label for logging (best available identifier) -->
        <set-variable name="caller-id" value="@{
            var azp = (string)context.Variables["claim-azp"];
            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(azp)) { return azp; }
            var appid = (string)context.Variables["claim-appid"];
            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(appid)) { return appid; }
            var mirid = (string)context.Variables["claim-xms_mirid"];
            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(mirid)) { return mirid; }
            var oid = (string)context.Variables["claim-oid"];
            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(oid)) { return oid; }
            return "unknown";
        }" />

        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <!-- STEP 3: Load contract config from blob (cached 5 min)            -->
        <!--                                                                  -->
        <!-- First request: fetches from Blob Storage via managed identity    -->
        <!-- Subsequent: reads from APIM internal cache (300s TTL)            -->
        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <cache-lookup-value key="contracts-blob-cache" variable-name="contracts-json" />
        <choose>
            <when condition="@(!context.Variables.ContainsKey("contracts-json"))">
                <send-request mode="new" response-variable-name="contracts-response" timeout="10" ignore-error="false">
                    <set-url>{contracts-blob-url}</set-url>
                    <set-method>GET</set-method>
                    <set-header name="x-ms-version" exists-action="override">
                        <value>2020-04-08</value>
                    </set-header>
                    <authentication-managed-identity resource="https://storage.azure.com/" />
                </send-request>
                <set-variable name="contracts-json" value="@(((IResponse)context.Variables["contracts-response"]).Body.As<string>())" />
                <cache-store-value key="contracts-blob-cache" value="@((string)context.Variables["contracts-json"])" duration="300" />
            </when>
        </choose>

        <!-- Parse caller's contract using identity prefix matching -->
        <!-- The blob has { contracts: {...}, identities: [{value, claimName, contract}, ...] } -->
        <set-variable name="contracts-parsed" value="@(Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject.Parse((string)context.Variables["contracts-json"]))" />
        <set-variable name="contract-match" value="@{
            try {
                var root = (Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject)context.Variables["contracts-parsed"];
                var identities = root["identities"] as Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JArray;
                if (identities == null) { return null; }

                // Build a dictionary of claim values for fast lookup
                var claims = new System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<string, string>();
                claims["azp"] = (string)context.Variables["claim-azp"];
                claims["appid"] = (string)context.Variables["claim-appid"];
                claims["xms_mirid"] = (string)context.Variables["claim-xms_mirid"];
                claims["oid"] = (string)context.Variables["claim-oid"];
                claims["sub"] = (string)context.Variables["claim-sub"];

                // Iterate identities, find first prefix match
                foreach (var identity in identities)
                {
                    var claimNameToken = identity["claimName"];
                    var prefixToken = identity["value"];
                    var contractToken = identity["contract"];

                    if (claimNameToken == null || prefixToken == null || contractToken == null) { continue; }

                    var claimName = claimNameToken.ToString();
                    var prefix = prefixToken.ToString();
                    var contractName = contractToken.ToString();

                    if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(claimName) || string.IsNullOrEmpty(prefix) || string.IsNullOrEmpty(contractName)) { continue; }

                    string claimValue;
                    if (!claims.TryGetValue(claimName, out claimValue) || string.IsNullOrEmpty(claimValue))
                    {
                        // azp (v2) and appid (v1) are equivalent — fall back across token versions
                        if (claimName == "azp") { claims.TryGetValue("appid", out claimValue); }
                        else if (claimName == "appid") { claims.TryGetValue("azp", out claimValue); }
                        if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(claimValue)) { continue; }
                    }

                    if (claimValue.StartsWith(prefix))
                    {
                        var displayToken = identity["displayName"];
                        var matchedName = displayToken != null ? displayToken.ToString() : prefix;
                        var contracts = root["contracts"] as Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject;
                        var contractObj = contracts != null ? contracts[contractName] : null;
                        var result = new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject();
                        result["contract"] = contractObj;
                        result["matchedIdentity"] = matchedName;
                        return result;
                    }
                }
                return null;
            } catch {
                return null;
            }
        }" />

        <set-variable name="contract-obj" value="@{
            var match = (Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject)context.Variables["contract-match"];
            if (match == null) { return null; }
            return match["contract"];
        }" />
        <set-variable name="matched-identity" value="@{
            var match = (Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject)context.Variables["contract-match"];
            if (match == null) { return "unknown"; }
            var v = match["matchedIdentity"];
            return v != null ? v.ToString() : "unknown";
        }" />

        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <!-- STEP 4: Authorization — reject unknown callers                   -->
        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <choose>
            <when condition="@(context.Variables["contract-obj"] == null)">
                <return-response>
                    <set-status code="403" reason="Forbidden" />
                    <set-header name="Content-Type" exists-action="override">
                        <value>application/json</value>
                    </set-header>
                    <set-body>@{
                        var callerId = (string)context.Variables["caller-id"];
                        return "{\"error\": \"forbidden\", \"message\": \"Identity '" + callerId + "' does not match any access contract. Contact the platform team to request access.\"}";
                    }</set-body>
                </return-response>
            </when>
        </choose>

        <!-- Extract contract fields into typed variables -->
        <set-variable name="caller-name" value="@{
            var c = (Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject)context.Variables["contract-obj"];
            var v = c["name"]; return v != null ? v.ToString() : "unknown";
        }" />
        <set-variable name="priority" value="@{
            var c = (Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject)context.Variables["contract-obj"];
            var v = c["priority"]; return v != null ? v.Value<int>() : 3;
        }" />
        <set-variable name="caller-monthly-quota" value="@{
            var c = (Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject)context.Variables["contract-obj"];
            var v = c["monthlyQuota"]; return v != null ? v.Value<long>() : (long)0;
        }" />
        <set-variable name="caller-environment" value="@{
            var c = (Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject)context.Variables["contract-obj"];
            var v = c["environment"]; return v != null ? v.ToString() : "UNKNOWN";
        }" />
        <set-variable name="contract-models" value="@(((Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject)context.Variables["contract-obj"])["models"])" />

        <!-- Derive priority label for headers/logging -->
        <set-variable name="priority-label" value="@{
            var p = (int)context.Variables["priority"];
            return p == 1 ? "production" : p == 2 ? "standard" : "economy";
        }" />

        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <!-- STEP 5: Extract requested model from URL path or request body   -->
        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <set-variable name="requestedModel" value="@{
            // Try URL pattern first: /openai/deployments/{model}/chat/completions
            var segments = context.Request.Url.Path.Split('/');
            for (int i = 0; i < segments.Length - 1; i++) {
                if (segments[i] == "deployments" && i + 1 < segments.Length) {
                    return segments[i + 1];
                }
            }
            // Fall back to request body 'model' field
            try {
                var body = context.Request.Body.As<Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject>(preserveContent: true);
                var model = body?["model"];
                if (model != null && !string.IsNullOrEmpty(model.ToString())) {
                    return model.ToString();
                }
            } catch { }
            return "unknown";
        }" />

        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <!-- STEP 6: Validate model access + extract per-model limits         -->
        <!--                                                                  -->
        <!-- The models object is { "gpt-4o": { tpm: 4000, ptuTpm: 2000 } }  -->
        <!-- If the requested model isn't in the map → 403.                   -->
        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <set-variable name="model-config" value="@{
            var models = (Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JToken)context.Variables["contract-models"];
            if (models == null) { return null; }
            var requested = (string)context.Variables["requestedModel"];
            var cfg = models[requested];
            return cfg;
        }" />

        <choose>
            <when condition="@(context.Variables["model-config"] == null)">
                <return-response>
                    <set-status code="403" reason="Forbidden" />
                    <set-header name="Content-Type" exists-action="override">
                        <value>application/json</value>
                    </set-header>
                    <set-body>@{
                        var model = (string)context.Variables["requestedModel"];
                        var models = (Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JToken)context.Variables["contract-models"];
                        var allowed = models != null ? string.Join(", ", ((Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject)models).Properties().Select(p => p.Name)) : "none";
                        return "{\"error\": \"model_not_authorized\", \"message\": \"Model '" + model + "' is not in your access contract. Allowed: " + allowed + "\"}";
                    }</set-body>
                </return-response>
            </when>
        </choose>

        <!-- Extract per-model TPM and PTU TPM from the model config -->
        <set-variable name="model-tpm" value="@{
            var m = (Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JToken)context.Variables["model-config"];
            var v = m["tpm"]; return v != null ? v.Value<int>() : 10000;
        }" />
        <set-variable name="model-ptu-tpm" value="@{
            var m = (Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JToken)context.Variables["model-config"];
            var v = m["ptuTpm"]; return v != null ? v.Value<int>() : 0;
        }" />

        <!-- Build allowed models list for response headers -->
        <set-variable name="allowed-models" value="@{
            var models = (Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JToken)context.Variables["contract-models"];
            if (models == null) { return ""; }
            return string.Join(",", ((Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject)models).Properties().Select(p => p.Name));
        }" />

        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <!-- STEP 7a: Enforce PER-MODEL TPM (hard limit)                      -->
        <!--                                                                  -->
        <!-- counter-key = contractName:model — all identities in a contract  -->
        <!-- share the same per-model TPM counter. This controls fair sharing -->
        <!-- of each model's capacity.                                        -->
        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <llm-token-limit
            counter-key="@((string)context.Variables["caller-name"] + ":" + (string)context.Variables["requestedModel"])"
            tokens-per-minute="@((int)context.Variables["model-tpm"])"
            estimate-prompt-tokens="true"
            remaining-tokens-header-name="x-ratelimit-remaining-tokens"
            tokens-consumed-header-name="x-tokens-consumed" />

        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <!-- STEP 7b: Enforce MONTHLY QUOTA across all models (cost cap)      -->
        <!--                                                                  -->
        <!-- counter-key = contractName — all identities + all models in a    -->
        <!-- contract share one monthly quota counter.                        -->
        <!-- tokens-per-minute set very high (not the real limit — that's 7a) -->
        <!-- Only the monthly token-quota matters here.                       -->
        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <llm-token-limit
            counter-key="@((string)context.Variables["caller-name"])"
            tokens-per-minute="10000000"
            token-quota="@((long)context.Variables["caller-monthly-quota"])"
            token-quota-period="Monthly"
            estimate-prompt-tokens="true"
            remaining-quota-tokens-header-name="x-quota-remaining-tokens" />

        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <!-- STEP 7c: Estimate token usage for pre-debit routing              -->
        <!--                                                                  -->
        <!-- Before forwarding, estimate how many tokens this request will    -->
        <!-- consume. This feeds the pre-debit mechanism: we immediately      -->
        <!-- increment the PTU cache so concurrent in-flight requests see     -->
        <!-- realistic utilization (not stale post-response values).          -->
        <!--                                                                  -->
        <!-- Estimation heuristic:                                            -->
        <!--   prompt_tokens  ≈ total_chars_in_messages / 4  (~4 chars/token) -->
        <!--   completion_est ≈ max_tokens / 2  (conservative midpoint)       -->
        <!--   total_estimate = prompt_tokens + completion_est                -->
        <!--                                                                  -->
        <!-- Outbound reconciles: replaces estimate with actual usage.        -->
        <!-- For streaming: estimate stands (SSE body can't be parsed).       -->
        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <set-variable name="is-streaming" value="@{
            try {
                var body = context.Request.Body.As<Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject>(preserveContent: true);
                var stream = body["stream"];
                return stream != null && stream.Value<bool>();
            } catch { return false; }
        }" />

        <set-variable name="estimated-tokens" value="@{
            try {
                var body = context.Request.Body.As<Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject>(preserveContent: true);
                var messages = body["messages"] as Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JArray;
                int promptChars = 0;
                if (messages != null) {
                    foreach (var msg in messages) {
                        promptChars += (msg["content"]?.ToString() ?? "").Length;
                    }
                }
                int promptTokens = Math.Max(promptChars / 4, 10);
                var maxTok = body["max_tokens"];
                int maxTokens = maxTok != null ? maxTok.Value<int>() : 100;
                int completionEstimate = Math.Max(maxTokens / 2, 10);
                return promptTokens + completionEstimate;
            } catch { return 50; }
        }" />

        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <!-- STEP 8: Three-Priority Routing with PTU Soft Limits              -->
        <!--                                                                  -->
        <!-- Two pools per model:                                             -->
        <!--   {model}-pool      — Mixed: PTU (priority 1) + PAYG (priority 2)-->
        <!--   {model}-payg-pool — PAYG-only (for overflow / economy)         -->
        <!--                                                                  -->
        <!-- Routing by contract priority:                                    -->
        <!--   P1 (production) → always {model}-pool → PTU first, circuit     -->
        <!--                     breaker handles 429 → PAYG fallback          -->
        <!--   P2 (standard)   → {model}-pool when utilization < threshold    -->
        <!--                     AND team has PTU quota; else {model}-payg-pool-->
        <!--   P3 (economy)    → always {model}-payg-pool (never uses PTU)    -->
        <!-- ================================================================ -->

        <!-- Read cached global PTU consumption -->
        <cache-lookup-value
            key="@("ptu-consumed-global:" + (string)context.Variables["requestedModel"])"
            variable-name="ptu-consumed-global-cached"
            default-value="0" />

        <!-- Read this team's PTU consumption in current window -->
        <cache-lookup-value
            key="@("ptu-consumed:" + (string)context.Variables["caller-name"] + ":" + (string)context.Variables["requestedModel"])"
            variable-name="ptu-consumed-cached"
            default-value="0" />

        <!-- Pre-debit: project consumption INCLUDING this request's estimate.       -->
        <!-- Write projected values to cache so concurrent requests see in-flight     -->
        <!-- usage. Outbound reconciles estimate → actual (or un-debits if PAYG).     -->
        <set-variable name="ptu-consumed-global-projected" value="@{
            int prev = 0;
            int.TryParse((string)context.Variables["ptu-consumed-global-cached"], out prev);
            return (int)context.Variables["priority"] <= 2
                ? prev + (int)context.Variables["estimated-tokens"]
                : prev;
        }" />
        <set-variable name="ptu-consumed-projected" value="@{
            int prev = 0;
            int.TryParse((string)context.Variables["ptu-consumed-cached"], out prev);
            return (int)context.Variables["priority"] <= 2
                ? prev + (int)context.Variables["estimated-tokens"]
                : prev;
        }" />

        <!-- Write pre-debited values for P1/P2 (P3 never uses PTU) -->
        <choose>
            <when condition="@((int)context.Variables["priority"] <= 2)">
                <cache-store-value
                    key="@("ptu-consumed-global:" + (string)context.Variables["requestedModel"])"
                    value="@(((int)context.Variables["ptu-consumed-global-projected"]).ToString())"
                    duration="60" />
                <cache-store-value
                    key="@("ptu-consumed:" + (string)context.Variables["caller-name"] + ":" + (string)context.Variables["requestedModel"])"
                    value="@(((int)context.Variables["ptu-consumed-projected"]).ToString())"
                    duration="60" />
            </when>
        </choose>

        <!-- PTU capacity is per-model, stored in the contracts blob -->
        <set-variable name="ptu-capacity" value="@{
            var root = (Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject)context.Variables["contracts-parsed"];
            var model = (string)context.Variables["requestedModel"];
            var ptuCap = root["ptuCapacity"] as Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject;
            if (ptuCap == null) { return 100000; }
            var val = ptuCap[model];
            if (val == null) { return 100000; }
            return val.Value<int>();
        }" />

        <!-- Calculate PTU utilization from PROJECTED consumption (includes pre-debit) -->
        <set-variable name="ptu-utilization" value="@{
            double consumed = (int)context.Variables["ptu-consumed-global-projected"];
            var capacity = (int)context.Variables["ptu-capacity"];
            if (capacity <= 0) { return 1.0; }
            var util = consumed / capacity;
            return util < 0.0 ? 0.0 : util > 1.0 ? 1.0 : util;
        }" />

        <!-- Check if team has PTU quota remaining (using PROJECTED per-team consumption) -->
        <set-variable name="ptu-quota-available" value="@{
            var ptuLimit = (int)context.Variables["model-ptu-tpm"];
            if (ptuLimit <= 0) { return false; }
            var consumed = (int)context.Variables["ptu-consumed-projected"];
            return consumed < ptuLimit;
        }" />

        <!-- P2 routing threshold (default 0.5 = 50%) -->
        <set-variable name="ptu-threshold" value="@{
            var t = "{ptu-utilization-threshold}";
            double val;
            return double.TryParse(t, out val) ? val : 0.5;
        }" />

        <!-- Build pool ID from model name (single pool per model) -->
        <set-variable name="model-clean" value="@(((string)context.Variables["requestedModel"]).Replace(".", "").Replace("-", ""))" />
        <set-variable name="pool-id" value="@((string)context.Variables["model-clean"] + "-pool")" />
        <set-variable name="payg-pool-id" value="@((string)context.Variables["model-clean"] + "-payg-pool")" />

        <!-- Priority routing decision -->
        <choose>
            <!-- P1 (production): PTU while soft limit not reached.                      -->
            <!-- When PTU 429s, <retry> in <backend> fails over to PAYG.                 -->
            <!-- When soft limit reached, go straight to PAYG.                            -->
            <when condition="@((int)context.Variables["priority"] == 1)">
                <choose>
                    <when condition="@((bool)context.Variables["ptu-quota-available"])">
                        <set-variable name="route-target" value="pool" />
                        <set-backend-service backend-id="@((string)context.Variables["pool-id"])" />
                    </when>
                    <otherwise>
                        <set-variable name="route-target" value="payg-overflow" />
                        <set-backend-service backend-id="@((string)context.Variables["payg-pool-id"])" />
                    </otherwise>
                </choose>
            </when>

            <!-- P2 (standard): PTU only when utilization < threshold AND team has quota -->
            <!-- Otherwise route to PAYG-only pool to preserve PTU for P1.               -->
            <when condition="@((int)context.Variables["priority"] == 2)">
                <choose>
                    <when condition="@((double)context.Variables["ptu-utilization"] < (double)context.Variables["ptu-threshold"] && (bool)context.Variables["ptu-quota-available"])">
                        <set-variable name="route-target" value="pool" />
                        <set-backend-service backend-id="@((string)context.Variables["pool-id"])" />
                    </when>
                    <otherwise>
                        <set-variable name="route-target" value="payg-overflow" />
                        <set-backend-service backend-id="@((string)context.Variables["payg-pool-id"])" />
                    </otherwise>
                </choose>
            </when>

            <!-- P3 (economy): Always route to PAYG-only pool — never touches PTU.      -->
            <otherwise>
                <set-variable name="route-target" value="payg" />
                <set-backend-service backend-id="@((string)context.Variables["payg-pool-id"])" />
            </otherwise>
        </choose>

        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <!-- STEP 9: Set tracing headers + backend auth                       -->
        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <set-header name="x-caller-id" exists-action="override">
            <value>@((string)context.Variables["caller-id"])</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-caller-identity" exists-action="override">
            <value>@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("matched-identity", "unknown"))</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-caller-name" exists-action="override">
            <value>@((string)context.Variables["caller-name"])</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-caller-priority" exists-action="override">
            <value>@((string)context.Variables["priority-label"])</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-route-target" exists-action="override">
            <value>@((string)context.Variables["route-target"])</value>
        </set-header>

        <!-- CORS for Azure AI Foundry portal -->
        <cors allow-credentials="true">
            <allowed-origins>
                <origin>https://ai.azure.com</origin>
            </allowed-origins>
            <allowed-methods preflight-result-max-age="300">
                <method>*</method>
            </allowed-methods>
            <allowed-headers>
                <header>*</header>
            </allowed-headers>
            <expose-headers>
                <header>*</header>
            </expose-headers>
        </cors>

        <!-- Authenticate to backend with APIM's managed identity -->
        <authentication-managed-identity resource="https://cognitiveservices.azure.com" output-token-variable-name="managed-id-access-token" ignore-error="false" />
        <set-header name="Authorization" exists-action="override">
            <value>@("Bearer " + (string)context.Variables["managed-id-access-token"])</value>
        </set-header>

        <!-- Emit token metrics with caller + routing dimensions -->
        <azure-openai-emit-token-metric namespace="openai">
            <dimension name="Caller ID" value="@((string)context.Variables["caller-id"])" />
            <dimension name="Caller Name" value="@((string)context.Variables["caller-name"])" />
            <dimension name="Priority" value="@((string)context.Variables["priority-label"])" />
            <dimension name="Route Target" value="@((string)context.Variables["route-target"])" />
            <dimension name="Model" value="@((string)context.Variables["requestedModel"])" />
            <dimension name="API ID" value="@(context.Api.Id)" />
        </azure-openai-emit-token-metric>
    </inbound>

    <backend>
        <!-- Forward to the backend chosen in inbound (pool or payg-pool).            -->
        <!-- If 429, retry once on the PAYG-only pool.                                -->
        <retry condition="@(context.Response != null && context.Response.StatusCode == 429)" count="2" interval="0" first-fast-retry="true">
            <choose>
                <when condition="@(context.Response != null && context.Response.StatusCode == 429)">
                    <set-backend-service backend-id="@((string)context.Variables["payg-pool-id"])" />
                </when>
            </choose>
            <forward-request buffer-request-body="true" />
        </retry>
    </backend>

    <outbound>
        <base />

        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <!-- Detect backend type: PTU or PAYG                                -->
        <!-- After forward-request through a pool, context.Request.Url.Host  -->
        <!-- reflects the actual backend that served the request.            -->
        <!-- {ptu-backend-hosts} is replaced at deploy time with a comma-    -->
        <!-- separated list of PTU endpoint hostnames.                       -->
        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <set-variable name="backend-type" value="@{
            var ptuHosts = "{ptu-backend-hosts}";
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(ptuHosts)) { return "payg"; }
            var host = context.Request.Url.Host.ToLower();
            foreach (var h in ptuHosts.Split(',')) {
                if (host == h.Trim().ToLower()) { return "ptu"; }
            }
            return "payg";
        }" />

        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <!-- Reconcile pre-debit: adjust PTU cache with actual vs estimated   -->
        <!--                                                                  -->
        <!-- Inbound pre-debited the cache with estimated tokens. Now:        -->
        <!--   PTU + non-streaming: reconcile (cache += actual - estimate)    -->
        <!--   PTU + streaming:     estimate stands (can't parse SSE body)    -->
        <!--   PAYG / error:        un-debit (cache -= estimate)             -->
        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <set-variable name="actual-tokens" value="@{
            if ((bool)context.Variables["is-streaming"]) { return 0; }
            try {
                var body = context.Response.Body.As<Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject>(preserveContent: true);
                var usage = body["usage"] as Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject;
                if (usage == null) { return 0; }
                var total = usage["total_tokens"];
                return total != null ? total.Value<int>() : 0;
            } catch { return 0; }
        }" />

        <choose>
            <!-- PTU non-streaming with actual data: reconcile estimate → actual -->
            <when condition="@((string)context.Variables["backend-type"] == "ptu" && (int)context.Variables["priority"] <= 2 && !(bool)context.Variables["is-streaming"] && (int)context.Variables["actual-tokens"] > 0)">
                <cache-lookup-value
                    key="@("ptu-consumed-global:" + (string)context.Variables["requestedModel"])"
                    variable-name="reconcile-global"
                    default-value="0" />
                <cache-store-value
                    key="@("ptu-consumed-global:" + (string)context.Variables["requestedModel"])"
                    value="@{
                        int current = 0;
                        int.TryParse((string)context.Variables["reconcile-global"], out current);
                        int adj = (int)context.Variables["actual-tokens"] - (int)context.Variables["estimated-tokens"];
                        return Math.Max(0, current + adj).ToString();
                    }"
                    duration="60" />
                <cache-lookup-value
                    key="@("ptu-consumed:" + (string)context.Variables["caller-name"] + ":" + (string)context.Variables["requestedModel"])"
                    variable-name="reconcile-team"
                    default-value="0" />
                <cache-store-value
                    key="@("ptu-consumed:" + (string)context.Variables["caller-name"] + ":" + (string)context.Variables["requestedModel"])"
                    value="@{
                        int current = 0;
                        int.TryParse((string)context.Variables["reconcile-team"], out current);
                        int adj = (int)context.Variables["actual-tokens"] - (int)context.Variables["estimated-tokens"];
                        return Math.Max(0, current + adj).ToString();
                    }"
                    duration="60" />
            </when>

            <!-- PAYG backend (P1/P2 only): un-debit — request didn't use PTU -->
            <when condition="@((string)context.Variables["backend-type"] == "payg" && (int)context.Variables["priority"] <= 2)">
                <cache-lookup-value
                    key="@("ptu-consumed-global:" + (string)context.Variables["requestedModel"])"
                    variable-name="undebit-global"
                    default-value="0" />
                <cache-store-value
                    key="@("ptu-consumed-global:" + (string)context.Variables["requestedModel"])"
                    value="@{
                        int current = 0;
                        int.TryParse((string)context.Variables["undebit-global"], out current);
                        return Math.Max(0, current - (int)context.Variables["estimated-tokens"]).ToString();
                    }"
                    duration="60" />
                <cache-lookup-value
                    key="@("ptu-consumed:" + (string)context.Variables["caller-name"] + ":" + (string)context.Variables["requestedModel"])"
                    variable-name="undebit-team"
                    default-value="0" />
                <cache-store-value
                    key="@("ptu-consumed:" + (string)context.Variables["caller-name"] + ":" + (string)context.Variables["requestedModel"])"
                    value="@{
                        int current = 0;
                        int.TryParse((string)context.Variables["undebit-team"], out current);
                        return Math.Max(0, current - (int)context.Variables["estimated-tokens"]).ToString();
                    }"
                    duration="60" />
            </when>

            <!-- PTU streaming: estimate stands (can't parse SSE body) — no action -->
        </choose>

        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <!-- Response headers for observability                               -->
        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <set-header name="x-ratelimit-limit-tokens" exists-action="override">
            <value>@(((int)context.Variables["model-tpm"]).ToString())</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-quota-limit-tokens" exists-action="override">
            <value>@(((long)context.Variables["caller-monthly-quota"]).ToString())</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-caller-name" exists-action="override">
            <value>@((string)context.Variables["caller-name"])</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-caller-identity" exists-action="override">
            <value>@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("matched-identity", "unknown"))</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-caller-priority" exists-action="override">
            <value>@((string)context.Variables["priority-label"])</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-route-target" exists-action="override">
            <value>@((string)context.Variables["route-target"])</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-ptu-utilization" exists-action="override">
            <value>@(((double)context.Variables["ptu-utilization"] * 100).ToString("F1") + "%")</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-ptu-consumed" exists-action="override">
            <value>@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("ptu-consumed-cached", "0"))</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-ptu-limit" exists-action="override">
            <value>@(((int)context.Variables["model-ptu-tpm"]).ToString())</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-actual-tokens" exists-action="override">
            <value>@(((int)context.Variables["actual-tokens"]).ToString())</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-estimated-tokens" exists-action="override">
            <value>@(((int)context.Variables["estimated-tokens"]).ToString())</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-is-streaming" exists-action="override">
            <value>@(((bool)context.Variables["is-streaming"]).ToString().ToLower())</value>
        </set-header>
        <set-header name="x-backend-type" exists-action="override">
            <value>@((string)context.Variables["backend-type"])</value>
        </set-header>
    </outbound>

    <on-error>
        <base />
        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <!-- EVENT HUB: Emit quota-exceeded event when monthly quota is hit   -->
        <!-- ================================================================ -->
        <choose>
            <when condition="@((context.LastError?.Message ?? "").Contains("quota"))">
                <log-to-eventhub logger-id="{eventhub-logger-id}">@{
                    var team   = (string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("caller-name", "unknown");
                    var model  = (string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("requestedModel", "unknown");
                    var msg    = context.LastError?.Message ?? "quota exceeded";
                    return new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject(
                        new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JProperty("event",     "quota_exceeded"),
                        new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JProperty("team",      team),
                        new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JProperty("model",     model),
                        new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JProperty("message",   msg),
                        new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JProperty("timestamp", DateTime.UtcNow.ToString("o")),
                        new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JProperty("requestId", context.RequestId.ToString())
                    ).ToString();
                }</log-to-eventhub>
            </when>
        </choose>
        <return-response>
            <set-status code="@((context.LastError?.Source == "validate-azure-ad-token") ? 401 : (context.Response != null ? context.Response.StatusCode : 500))" reason="@(context.LastError?.Reason ?? (context.Response != null ? context.Response.StatusReason : "Internal Server Error"))" />
            <set-header name="Content-Type" exists-action="override">
                <value>application/json</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-header name="x-error-reason" exists-action="override">
                <value>@(context.LastError?.Reason ?? "unknown")</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-header name="x-error-message" exists-action="override">
                <value>@(context.LastError?.Message ?? "unknown")</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-header name="x-error-section" exists-action="override">
                <value>@(context.LastError?.Section ?? "unknown")</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-header name="x-error-source" exists-action="override">
                <value>@(context.LastError?.Source ?? "unknown")</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-header name="x-caller-id" exists-action="override">
                <value>@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("caller-id", "unknown"))</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-header name="x-caller-name" exists-action="override">
                <value>@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("caller-name", "unknown"))</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-header name="x-caller-identity" exists-action="override">
                <value>@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("matched-identity", "unknown"))</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-header name="x-caller-priority" exists-action="override">
                <value>@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("priority-label", "unknown"))</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-header name="x-route-target" exists-action="override">
                <value>@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("route-target", "unknown"))</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-header name="x-ratelimit-limit-tokens" exists-action="override">
                <value>@(context.Variables.ContainsKey("model-tpm") ? ((int)context.Variables["model-tpm"]).ToString() : "0")</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-header name="x-ratelimit-remaining-tokens" exists-action="override">
                <value>@(context.Variables.ContainsKey("tpm-remaining") ? ((int)context.Variables["tpm-remaining"]).ToString() : "0")</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-header name="x-quota-limit-tokens" exists-action="override">
                <value>@(context.Variables.ContainsKey("caller-monthly-quota") ? ((long)context.Variables["caller-monthly-quota"]).ToString() : "0")</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-header name="x-quota-remaining-tokens" exists-action="override">
                <value>@(context.Variables.ContainsKey("quota-remaining") ? ((int)context.Variables["quota-remaining"]).ToString() : "0")</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-header name="x-ptu-consumed" exists-action="override">
                <value>@(context.Variables.ContainsKey("ptu-consumed-projected") ? ((int)context.Variables["ptu-consumed-projected"]).ToString() : "0")</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-header name="x-ptu-limit" exists-action="override">
                <value>@(context.Variables.ContainsKey("model-ptu-tpm") ? ((int)context.Variables["model-ptu-tpm"]).ToString() : "0")</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-header name="x-ptu-utilization" exists-action="override">
                <value>@(context.Variables.ContainsKey("ptu-utilization") ? ((double)context.Variables["ptu-utilization"]).ToString("F2") : "0.00")</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-header name="x-estimated-tokens" exists-action="override">
                <value>@(context.Variables.ContainsKey("estimated-tokens") ? ((int)context.Variables["estimated-tokens"]).ToString() : "0")</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-body>@{
                var source  = context.LastError?.Source ?? "";
                var reason  = context.LastError?.Reason ?? "unknown";
                var rawMsg  = context.LastError?.Message ?? "";
                var team    = (string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("caller-name", "unknown");
                var model   = (string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("requestedModel", "unknown");

                string code;
                string message;

                if (source == "validate-azure-ad-token")
                {
                    code = "unauthorized";
                    message = "Invalid or missing bearer token. Provide a valid Entra ID token with audience https://cognitiveservices.azure.com.";
                }
                else if (rawMsg.Contains("quota"))
                {
                    var quota = context.Variables.ContainsKey("caller-monthly-quota")
                        ? ((long)context.Variables["caller-monthly-quota"]).ToString("N0")
                        : "unknown";
                    code = "monthly_quota_exceeded";
                    message = "Monthly token quota exhausted for '" + team + "'. "
                            + "Your contract allows " + quota + " tokens per month across all models. "
                            + "Contact the platform team to request a quota increase.";
                }
                else if (source == "llm-token-limit")
                {
                    var tpm = context.Variables.ContainsKey("model-tpm")
                        ? ((int)context.Variables["model-tpm"]).ToString("N0")
                        : "unknown";
                    code = "rate_limit_exceeded";
                    message = "Per-model rate limit exceeded for '" + team + "' on model '" + model + "'. "
                            + "Your contract allows " + tpm + " tokens per minute for this model. "
                            + "Wait a moment and retry, or contact the platform team to request a higher limit.";
                }
                else
                {
                    code = reason;
                    message = !string.IsNullOrEmpty(rawMsg) ? rawMsg : "An error occurred processing the request.";
                }

                return new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject(
                    new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JProperty("error", new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject(
                        new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JProperty("code", code),
                        new Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JProperty("message", message)
                    ))
                ).ToString();
            }</set-body>
        </return-response>
    </on-error>
</policies>
variables.$fxv#1:
<policies>
    <inbound>
        <base />

        <!-- Fetch contracts JSON from Blob Storage (same blob as the routing policy uses) -->
        <send-request mode="new" response-variable-name="contracts-response" timeout="10" ignore-error="false">
            <set-url>{contracts-blob-url}</set-url>
            <set-method>GET</set-method>
            <set-header name="x-ms-version" exists-action="override">
                <value>2020-04-08</value>
            </set-header>
            <authentication-managed-identity resource="https://storage.azure.com/" />
        </send-request>

        <set-variable name="contracts-json" value="@(((IResponse)context.Variables["contracts-response"]).Body.As<string>())" />

        <!-- Render as styled HTML -->
        <return-response>
            <set-status code="200" reason="OK" />
            <set-header name="Content-Type" exists-action="override">
                <value>text/html; charset=utf-8</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-body>@{
    var json = (string)context.Variables["contracts-json"];
    var root = Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject.Parse(json);
    var contracts = root["contracts"] as Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject;
    var identities = root["identities"] as Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JArray;
    var rows = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
    var now = System.DateTime.UtcNow.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss UTC");

    int totalContracts = 0;
    int p1Count = 0, p2Count = 0, p3Count = 0;
    long totalMonthly = 0;

    if (contracts != null) {
        foreach (var prop in contracts.Properties())
        {
            totalContracts++;
            var c = (Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject)prop.Value;
            var nameToken = c["name"];
            var name = nameToken != null ? nameToken.ToString() : "—";
            var priToken = c["priority"];
            var priority = priToken != null ? priToken.Value<int>() : 3;
            var monthlyToken = c["monthlyQuota"];
            var monthly = monthlyToken != null ? monthlyToken.Value<long>() : (long)0;
            var envToken = c["environment"];
            var env = envToken != null ? envToken.ToString() : "—";
            var models = c["models"] as Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject;

            if (priority == 1) { p1Count++; }
            else if (priority == 2) { p2Count++; }
            else { p3Count++; }
            totalMonthly += monthly;

            var priBadge = priority == 1
                ? "<span class='badge p1'>P1 Production</span>"
                : priority == 2
                    ? "<span class='badge p2'>P2 Standard</span>"
                    : "<span class='badge p3'>P3 Economy</span>";
            var envBadge = env == "PROD"
                ? "<span class='env prod'>" + env + "</span>"
                : "<span class='env dev'>" + env + "</span>";

            // Build identities sub-rows
            var idRows = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
            var contractIdentities = c["identities"] as Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JArray;
            if (contractIdentities != null)
            {
                foreach (var id in contractIdentities)
                {
                    var dnToken = id["displayName"];
                    var dn = dnToken != null ? dnToken.ToString() : "—";
                    var valToken = id["value"];
                    var val = valToken != null ? valToken.ToString() : "—";
                    var claimToken = id["claimName"];
                    var claim = claimToken != null ? claimToken.ToString() : "—";
                    idRows.Append("<div class='id-row'><strong>" + dn + "</strong> ");
                    idRows.Append("<span class='claim-badge'>" + claim + "</span> ");
                    idRows.Append("<code class='appid'>" + val + "</code></div>");
                }
            }

            // Build per-model sub-rows
            var modelRows = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
            if (models != null)
            {
                foreach (var mp in models.Properties())
                {
                    var mObj = (Newtonsoft.Json.Linq.JObject)mp.Value;
                    var tpmToken = mObj["tpm"];
                    var tpm = tpmToken != null ? tpmToken.Value<long>() : (long)0;
                    var ptuToken = mObj["ptuTpm"];
                    var ptuTpm = ptuToken != null ? ptuToken.Value<long>() : (long)0;
                    var ptuBar = tpm > 0 && ptuTpm > 0
                        ? "<div class='bar-bg'><div class='bar-fill' style='width:" + (ptuTpm * 100 / tpm) + "%'></div></div>"
                        : "";
                    modelRows.Append("<div class='model-row'><code>" + mp.Name + "</code> ");
                    modelRows.Append("<span class='num'>" + tpm.ToString("N0") + " TPM</span>");
                    if (ptuTpm > 0) {
                        var ptuLabel = ptuTpm == tpm ? "PTU TPM (limit)" : "PTU TPM (soft limit)";
                        modelRows.Append(" <span class='ptu-badge'>" + ptuTpm.ToString("N0") + " " + ptuLabel + "</span>");
                    }
                    modelRows.Append(ptuBar + "</div>");
                }
            }

        rows.Append("<tr>");
        rows.Append("<td><strong>" + name + "</strong></td>");
        rows.Append("<td>" + idRows.ToString() + "</td>");
        rows.Append("<td>" + priBadge + "</td>");
        rows.Append("<td>" + modelRows.ToString() + "</td>");
        rows.Append("<td class='num'>" + monthly.ToString("N0") + "</td>");
        rows.Append("<td>" + envBadge + "</td>");
        rows.Append("</tr>");
        }
    }

    return @"<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang='en'>
<head>
<meta charset='utf-8'>
<meta name='viewport' content='width=device-width, initial-scale=1'>
<title>AI Gateway — Access Contracts</title>
<style>
  :root { --bg: #0d1117; --card: #161b22; --border: #30363d; --text: #e6edf3;
           --muted: #8b949e; --p1: #f97583; --p2: #d2a8ff; --p3: #79c0ff;
           --prod: #3fb950; --dev: #58a6ff; --accent: #58a6ff; }
  * { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }
  body { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;
         background: var(--bg); color: var(--text); padding: 2rem; line-height: 1.5; }
  h1 { font-size: 1.5rem; margin-bottom: .25rem; }
  .subtitle { color: var(--muted); font-size: .85rem; margin-bottom: 1.5rem; }
  .stats { display: flex; gap: 1rem; margin-bottom: 1.5rem; flex-wrap: wrap; }
  .stat { background: var(--card); border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 8px;
          padding: .75rem 1.25rem; min-width: 130px; }
  .stat .label { color: var(--muted); font-size: .75rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .05em; }
  .stat .value { font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 600; }
  table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; background: var(--card);
          border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 8px; overflow: hidden; }
  th { background: #1c2129; color: var(--muted); font-size: .75rem; text-transform: uppercase;
       letter-spacing: .05em; padding: .75rem 1rem; text-align: left; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border); }
  td { padding: .75rem 1rem; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border); font-size: .875rem; vertical-align: top; }
  tr:last-child td { border-bottom: none; }
  tr:hover { background: #1c2129; }
  .num { font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; text-align: right; font-family: 'SF Mono', Consolas, monospace; }
  .badge { display: inline-block; padding: .15em .6em; border-radius: 12px; font-size: .75rem;
           font-weight: 600; white-space: nowrap; }
  .p1 { background: rgba(249,117,131,.15); color: var(--p1); border: 1px solid rgba(249,117,131,.3); }
  .p2 { background: rgba(210,168,255,.15); color: var(--p2); border: 1px solid rgba(210,168,255,.3); }
  .p3 { background: rgba(121,192,255,.15); color: var(--p3); border: 1px solid rgba(121,192,255,.3); }
  .env { display: inline-block; padding: .1em .5em; border-radius: 4px; font-size: .7rem;
         font-weight: 600; text-transform: uppercase; }
  .prod { background: rgba(63,185,80,.15); color: var(--prod); }
  .dev { background: rgba(88,166,255,.15); color: var(--dev); }
  .appid { font-size: .7rem; color: var(--muted); word-break: break-all; }
  .id-row { margin-bottom: 4px; }
  .claim-badge { display: inline-block; padding: .05em .4em; border-radius: 4px; font-size: .65rem;
                 font-weight: 600; background: rgba(139,148,158,.15); color: var(--muted); font-family: 'SF Mono', Consolas, monospace; }
  .model-row { margin-bottom: 4px; }
  .model-row code { font-size: .8rem; }
  .ptu-badge { display: inline-block; padding: .05em .4em; border-radius: 4px; font-size: .7rem;
               font-weight: 600; background: rgba(88,166,255,.15); color: var(--accent); }
  .bar-bg { margin-top: 2px; height: 3px; background: var(--border); border-radius: 2px; }
  .bar-fill { height: 100%; background: var(--accent); border-radius: 2px; transition: width .3s; }
  footer { margin-top: 1.5rem; color: var(--muted); font-size: .75rem; }
  code { background: rgba(110,118,129,.15); padding: .1em .3em; border-radius: 3px; font-size: .8em; }
  @media (max-width: 800px) { body { padding: 1rem; } td, th { padding: .5rem; } }
</style>
</head>
<body>
  <h1>🛡️ AI Gateway — Access Contracts</h1>
  <p class='subtitle'>JWT Citadel with 3-Priority PTU Routing &middot; Per-Model TPM</p>

  <div class='stats'>
    <div class='stat'><div class='label'>Teams</div><div class='value'>" + totalContracts + @"</div></div>
    <div class='stat'><div class='label'>P1 Prod</div><div class='value' style='color:var(--p1)'>" + p1Count + @"</div></div>
    <div class='stat'><div class='label'>P2 Standard</div><div class='value' style='color:var(--p2)'>" + p2Count + @"</div></div>
    <div class='stat'><div class='label'>P3 Economy</div><div class='value' style='color:var(--p3)'>" + p3Count + @"</div></div>
    <div class='stat'><div class='label'>Total Monthly</div><div class='value'>" + totalMonthly.ToString("N0") + @"</div></div>
  </div>

  <table>
    <thead>
      <tr>
        <th>Team</th>
        <th>Identities</th>
        <th>Priority</th>
        <th>Models (TPM / PTU)</th>
        <th style='text-align:right'>Monthly Quota</th>
        <th>Env</th>
      </tr>
    </thead>
    <tbody>" + rows.ToString() + @"</tbody>
  </table>

  <footer>
    Generated at " + now + @" by APIM policy &middot; Source: Blob Storage &middot;
    Cached 5 min &middot; <code>GET /gateway/config</code>
  </footer>
</body>
</html>";
            }</set-body>
        </return-response>
    </inbound>
    <backend>
        <base />
    </backend>
    <outbound>
        <base />
    </outbound>
    <on-error>
        <base />
        <return-response>
            <set-status code="500" reason="Internal Server Error" />
            <set-header name="Content-Type" exists-action="override">
                <value>text/html; charset=utf-8</value>
            </set-header>
            <set-body>@{
                var err = context.LastError;
                var msg = err != null ? err.Message : "Unknown error loading contracts";
                return "<html><body style='font-family:sans-serif;padding:2rem;background:#0d1117;color:#e6edf3'><h1>⚠️ Error loading contracts</h1><pre>" + msg + "</pre></body></html>";
            }</set-body>
        </return-response>
    </on-error>
</policies>
